reasoning: The 6-month T-bill rate has declined over the past year from 4.33% to 3.65%. The most recent data point (3.65 on 10-24) is slightly below the recent 30-day regression trend line. The trend analysis tool projects a bounce above 3.65 by Nov 2 (P=0.64) mainly due to short-term mean reversion. However, over the longer span looking out to late November and January 2026, the ongoing structural downward trend propelled by an easing Fed makes a lower value more likely. In adherence to the instruction that uncertainty should increase for distant dates (moving probabilities closer to 0.5), my estimates progressively move closer to 50% for the subsequent horizons.
